response:
A 

The question requires identifying a material that is flexible/malleable enough to be bent by hand into a circular shape without breaking or requiring special tools. Copper (option A) is a ductile metal that is commonly drawn into thin, easily manipulable wire forms; this makes it straightforward to bend into a circle (e.g., as in basic science experiments modeling orbits or circuits). 

By contrast: glass (B) is brittle and snaps under bending stress; lead pipe (C) is made of a soft metal but is typically too thick/rigid in pipe form to bend easily without equipment (and poses safety issues for students); wood (D) is stiff and prone to splintering or breaking rather than curving smoothly. This leaves A as the only practical choice based on standard material properties.
